results.title: For investors with 1- to 2-year perspective and ability to ignore volatility: 6 stocks with an upside potential of up to 26%
results.description: The US-Iran war is escalating again, with all that means for crude prices and nervous markets. And halfway across the world, the long AI trade is unwinding, dragging down the high-priced names that led the rally. Between the two, volatility, which has been the defining feature of 2026, is set to rise again. But volatility has a shelf life. It comes, it frightens, and it goes. What survives it is the underlying business, the quality of the management running it, and their proven ability to handle exactly this kind of complexity.

results.title: Forest Service Employees Held Hostage Near Mount Shasta, Father and Son Charged
results.description: Two Forest Service employees rescued after 15 hours held hostage near Mount Shasta; father and son face federal kidnapping charges. Joseph Henrichsen, 49, and Phoenix Henrichsen, 23, surrendered at 2:30 a.m. Friday following FBI crisis negotiations at Shasta-Trinity National Forest.

results.title: Magalong warns of fundraising scam

results.title: Tate Brothers Arrested in Miami as UK Files Additional Rape and Trafficking Charges
results.description: U.S. Marshals arrested Andrew and Tristan Tate in Miami on Saturday as UK prosecutors filed new rape and trafficking charges covering four additional victims and offenses dating to 2010. The warrant was sealed, with U.S. Marshals unable to confirm the specific charges publicly.

results.title: Mbappe Breaks All-Time World Cup Scoring Record as England Beat France 6-4 in Miami
results.description: Kylian Mbappe scored twice to push his World Cup total to 22 and break Lionel Messi's all-time record, but England won the bronze medal match 6-4 after Bukayo Saka's hat-trick dominated the first half. Jude Bellingham also set a new England record with his seventh tournament goal in Miami Gardens.

results.title: World News | Death Toll from Venezuela's Twin Earthquakes Rises Above 5,000 as IMF Announces Emergency Funds
results.description: Get latest articles and stories on World at LatestLY. The death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month has risen to 5,119, according to an official government report, as the International Monetary Fund (IMF) announced emergency financial support of USD 346 million to assist recovery and reconstruction efforts.
